behaviors.description: History sent to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a handoff behavior between history management and the LLM request builder, so unit-only can miss wiring bugs. REAL-INFRA: no explicit real HTTP/OS/subprocess contract check is required. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model judgment, only on payload contents. Rejected System/Agentic because the behavior text is about "history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages," not external response contracts or output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the chat pipeline in-process with real collaborating modules, submit >10 prior messages, and assert the outbound LLM payload contains only the latest 10 in order.

behaviors.description: Generated response answers the customer’s actual question (not generic/off-topic).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is directly about response relevance/quality, which a stub cannot validate. MOCK-HIDE: mocked outputs would always pass if fixture is good. MULTI-STEP: behavior describes one generation quality check, not a required multi-agentic chain. Rejected Workflow because the text only requires the generated answer to be on-topic, not a sequence of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model calls on a fixed prompt set and evaluate whether responses are question-specific and on-topic via rubric/scoring thresholds.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects an overly formal reply for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans multiple agentic steps in sequence: initial generation, tone judgment, then regeneration trigger/second generation. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(d): orchestration across LLM-involved stages should not stay Integration when model decisions govern handoff correctness. Rejected Agentic as too low because the behavior explicitly includes both detection and regeneration sequencing, not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real end-to-end tone pipeline with a casual-tier scenario, observe a first too-formal draft, verify tone-check flags it, and confirm a regeneration step is invoked and completed.

behaviors.description: Entire exchange (user message, response, regenerations) is saved with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: persistence shape/order across application + repository + DB can fail in wiring. Integration level includes local DB/filesystem and is sufficient here. REAL-INFRA/System triad: behavior text does not require validating an external HTTP/subprocess contract; it requires DB persistence correctness. Rejected System because no explicit external API/exit-code/error-shape contract is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Run request handling against a real test database, then read back stored records and timestamps to verify message, draft/regeneration entries, and temporal ordering.

behaviors.description: If regeneration is triggered, the second response replaces the first and only the final response is customer-visible.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD #3: success is an externally visible side effect (what the customer sees), so it should be proven via round-trip at the user-facing boundary. MOCK-MISS: in-process checks can miss serialization/handler leaks exposing the rejected draft. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because the behavior text is about visibility/replacement semantics once regeneration occurs ("When the tone check triggers regeneration"), not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the real customer-facing interface (e.g., HTTP/chat endpoint) in a scenario where regeneration occurs, then verify the delivered response is only the second draft and the first draft is never returned in the outward payload/stream.
